title: After-Action Report — 2C resolves through the hitting engine
scope: Stage 1 + Stage 2 shipped & validated. Balance is tunable via `config.py` `STAY_*`; see follow-ups.

rationale.item: It started as a wOBA seam (the league baseline still used MLB weights). Fixing that surfaced the `stay_hits ⊆ singles` assumption, which the owner flagged as flat-out wrong: "a 2C hit can be anything — why wouldn't it be?" Pulling that thread exposed that the 2C mechanic was structurally broken, and the owner chose to fix it properly with heavy documentation of the learnings.

Root-cause findings: `pa.py:902` forced `batter_safe = True` on every valid stay; `prob.py` overrode `runner_advances` with a flat talent-gated `[adv,adv,adv]`; `render.py` credited `s.hits += 1` (a single) regardless of type; no max-3 cap; wOBA derivation bucketed a 2C as a separate STAY type while counting stats credited it a single.

delegation_events.decision: Reverse prior documented 2C design and fix the mechanic properly
delegation_events.quote: The README and `aar-2c-reframe-and-shifts.md` framed 2C as "a runner-advancement tool, not a hit-creation tool," "multiple stays per AB, no cap," batter "stays at the plate," and "caught fly is the only out." The owner evolved all of that.

delegation_events.decision: 2C decision must be EV-driven exclusively, no frequency knobs
delegation_events.quote: EV-driven, exclusively (owner: "2C should be EV-driven exclusively"). No `stay_aggressiveness` frequency knobs.

delegation_events.decision: 2C is a RISP tool; high risk must pay off; skill-differentiated with jokers leveraging it most
delegation_events.quote: High risk, must pay off, don't waste a good hit (owner) ... 2C is a RISP tool (owner) ... Skill-differentiated (owner)

delegation_events.decision: 2C wOBA credit: credit a 2C advancement like the hit a runner-side batter would produce; exclude 2C events from the run-value fit
"Max 3 batted balls" implemented as strike-gated; any fielding out retires the batter; EV is a heuristic not a true RE calc
delegation_events.quote: Design decisions I had to infer (flagged for the owner)

actions.description: Stage 1 — engine + renderer: stay_available caps at strikes < 2; stay_results_in_out retires batter on any fielding out; deleted [adv,adv,adv] override; should_stay_prob rewritten EV-driven; stay branch routes outs to batter-out path; every stay burns a strike; replay mirrors new out rule; 2C hits credit real type; new STAY_* EV constants

Stage 2 — wOBA reconciliation: _classify_bip buckets a credited 2C by hit_type (no STAY bucket); STAY removed from weight construction and league-wOBA build; app.py dropped the singles − stay_hits split + STAY weight in per-player aggregator and league baseline

2C cost side: new stat c2_strand_out (BatterStats + game_batter_stats, incremented in render's batter-out paths when ab_hits_before > 0); wOBA numerator subtracts ww["1B"] × c2_strand_out in per-player aggregator and league baseline

risk_posture: EV is a heuristic, not a true RE calc — the engine has no run-expectancy matrix at sim time.

validation_claims.claim: Run environment preserved. Standalone 8.9 R/half (5 seeds); DB sims 11.4 R/half — both near O27's ~10 baseline.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-sim with same seeds and compare R/half

validation_claims.claim: 2C behaves as specified: 1.12 stays/game, 100% with RISP, 87% with multiple runners in scoring position
validation_claims.check_method: Re-sim and aggregate stay events by base state
Run python -m o27.main --seed N twice and diff output

validation_claims.claim: PA == AB+BB+HBP+SH holds; o27/tests 106 pass; o27v2/tests 130 pass; wRC+ centers ~101, league wOBA≈OBP, woba_weights has no STAY and keeps RV(1B)>RV(BB). Stat invariants 11/12.
validation_claims.check_method: Run pytest o27/tests o27v2/tests and tests/test_stat_invariants.py

validation_claims.claim: On a 200-game sim, 2C attempt-rate correlates +0.19 with hitting skill (contact+eye)
validation_claims.check_method: Sim 200 games and compute correlation of 2C attempt rate vs contact+eye

validation_claims.claim: Probing should_stay_prob directly: stay-rate climbs poor 16% → avg 33% → good 44% → elite 55% (medium contact, RISP); jokers poor 23% → elite 81%; run env 11.95 R/half, 2C 1.23/game
validation_claims.check_method: Probe should_stay_prob across skill tiers in a script

validation_claims.claim: wRC+ centers at exactly 100 after run-value fit exclusion; c2_strand_out ~0.6/game; wRC+ stays centered at 100
validation_claims.check_method: Re-sim and check league wRC+ mean

non_actions.reason: Defense-read was retained but decoupled from the old advance gate; its balance should be re-checked.
league wERA 10.47 vs raw ERA 10.53 — diff 0.062 vs the 0.05 tolerance ... A small wERA-fit retune (separate subsystem), not a 2C correctness bug.

non_actions.item: Sharpening the per-player success-rate delta and joker lean as a tuning follow-up
non_actions.reason: The per-player success-rate delta isn't cleanly measurable at the current 2C rarity (~1/game → too few stays per player)

residual_risks: Stat invariants 11/12 — the one miss is the wERA outs-weighted reconstruction on a 40-game DB (league wERA==ERA is exact); rechecked on a larger sim.
wERA outs-weighted invariant is borderline: diff 0.062 vs the 0.05 tolerance; the fixed wERA arc weights (0.85/1.00/1.20) are slightly loose under the 2C-shifted arc distribution.
open_hooks: STAY_REWARD_*, STAY_OUT_RISK_*, STAY_RUN_BASELINE_* are single-knob levers for 2C frequency, the RISP focus, the skill delta, and XBH rate
The c2_strand_out penalty value (one single-weight) is tunable
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — 2C resolves through the hitting engine
3. The mechanic now (owner spec, captured across the session)
6. Design decisions I had to infer (flagged for the owner)
unmapped_content: Learning: the stat read 0 at first because the per-phase delta helper `render._stat_delta` carries an explicit field list — any new BatterStats counter must be added there or it's silently zeroed before `o27v2/sim.py` persists it. (Same trap applies to the migration list in `o27v2/db.py` and both INSERT column/placeholder lists.) | Mechanic spec (section 3): A 2C AB = at most 3 batted balls; each stay burns a strike; stay available only at strikes < 2. Resolves through the real hitting engine: hit non-HR → batter stays, runners advance by real type; fielding out → batter OUT, AB ends (sac fly possible) — 'This is the pesäpallo conflict, finally resolved'; HR → batter runs (Walk-Back), never stays. | Header metadata: Branch claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG; Commits a724e80 (spec) → 4770f1a (Stage 1 mechanic) → a26ac85 (EV tuning) → 9eaf3a9 (Stage 2 wOBA). | Before the EV rewrite, a naïve port cratered offense to 0–1 R/half — outs are now a real 2C risk; the EV decision is the balance fix.
parser_notes: Several '§7 Follow-ups / not done' items are marked DONE inline (the doc was updated after initial writing), so the follow-ups section mixes completed and deferred work; DONE items recorded under actions, remaining ones under non_actions.
